search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk cg183 drug allergy assessment structured documentation
candidate_sources_rejected: anaphylaxis treatment algorithm and medicine doses
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The diagnosis and allergen remain clinician-established.
Emergency treatment and dosing were reviewed only for exclusion and were not mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with a suspected allergic reaction; exact support is strongest for suspected medicine-related reactions, while food, venom, contact and idiopathic reactions remain less directly covered.
setting_applicability: Emergency documentation and subsequent allergy-record communication.
UAE_applicability: Clinical documentation concepts transfer, but UAE allergy referral, pharmacovigilance, formulary and emergency pathways apply.
recency_verification: Current NICE CG183 and RCUK 2021 official sources were reviewed on 2026-07-14; CG183 age remains a recency limitation.
superseded_check: NICE CG183 remains current and RCUK 2021 remains the reviewed anaphylaxis guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE drug-allergy section supports documenting chronology, associated features, severity and relevant negatives when a medicine exposure is suspected; it does not establish allergy or generalize to every allergen.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting history, examination, timing after exposure, erythema, urticaria, angioedema, respiratory or cardiovascular features, macules, papules, fixed eruption, fever, lymphadenopathy, mucosal erosion, vesicles, blistering, detachment, pustules, photosensitivity, and vasculitic features without asserting a diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact structured-record section supports a clinician-entered record of the reaction and plan, without automatically labelling an allergy or recommending treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Anaphylaxis recognition was reviewed to delimit severe airway, breathing or circulation presentations; it does not convert this broad allergic-reaction workflow into an anaphylaxis diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Non-drug triggers, exposure details, morphology, airway/breathing/circulation findings, diagnosis, tests, treatment, observation, discharge, referral and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
